options.text: The relationship between force applied to a spring and how much it stretches

options.label: B
options.text: How springs lose their elasticity over time

options.label: C
options.text: The maximum weight a spring can support

options.label: D
options.text: How to calculate the speed of a bouncing spring

explanation: Hooke's Law states that the extension of a spring is directly proportional to the force applied to it, as long as the spring is not permanently deformed (within its elastic limit).
